title: Amazon SQS Message Schema
description: JSON Schema defining the structure of Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) messages, including standard messages, FIFO messages, batch operations, dead-letter queue messages, and Lambda event source mapping payloads.

MessageAttributeValue.title: Message Attribute Value
MessageAttributeValue.description: The value of a custom message attribute. SQS supports String, Number, and Binary data types for message attributes.

StringValue.description: Strings are Unicode with UTF-8 binary encoding. For the Number data type, use StringValue to send a numeric value.

BinaryValue.description: Binary type attributes can store any binary data, such as compressed data, encrypted data, or images.

StringListValues.description: Not implemented. Reserved for future use.

BinaryListValues.description: Not implemented. Reserved for future use.

DataType.description: Amazon SQS supports String, Number, and Binary data types. Custom data types using the format 'Type.CustomType' are also supported.

MessageSystemAttribute.title: Message System Attribute
MessageSystemAttribute.description: System attributes that can be set on a message at send time.

AWSTraceHeader.description: The X-Ray trace header string for distributed tracing.

RedrivePolicy.title: Redrive Policy
RedrivePolicy.description: The redrive policy configuration for a queue, specifying the dead-letter queue and maximum receive count.

deadLetterTargetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the dead-letter queue to which messages are moved after maxReceiveCount is exceeded.

maxReceiveCount.description: The number of times a message can be received before being moved to the dead-letter queue. Valid values are 1 to 1000.

SQSMessage.title: SQS Message
SQSMessage.description: An Amazon SQS message as returned by the ReceiveMessage API action. Contains the message body, system attributes, custom attributes, and metadata needed for message processing.

MessageId.description: A unique identifier for the message. A MessageId is considered unique across all AWS accounts for an extended period of time.

ReceiptHandle.description: An identifier associated with the act of receiving the message. A new receipt handle is returned every time you receive a message. When deleting a message, you provide the last received receipt handle.

MD5OfBody.description: An MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message body string.

MD5OfMessageAttributes.description: An MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message attribute string. Returned only if message attributes were requested.

Attributes.description: A map of the system attributes requested in ReceiveMessage.

SenderId.description: For an IAM user, returns the IAM user ID. For an IAM role, returns the IAM role ID.

SentTimestamp.description: The time the message was sent to the queue (epoch time in milliseconds).

ApproximateReceiveCount.description: The number of times a message has been received across all queues but not deleted.

ApproximateFirstReceiveTimestamp.description: The time the message was first received from the queue (epoch time in milliseconds).

SequenceNumber.description: The large, non-consecutive number that Amazon SQS assigns to each message in FIFO queues.

MessageDeduplicationId.description: The deduplication ID provided when the message was sent to a FIFO queue.

MessageGroupId.description: The message group ID provided when the message was sent to a FIFO queue.

DeadLetterQueueSourceArn.description: The ARN of the source queue in case the message comes from a dead-letter queue redrive.

MessageAttributes.description: Each message attribute consists of a Name, Type, and Value.

SendMessageRequest.title: Send Message Request
SendMessageRequest.description: The request payload for sending a single message to an SQS queue.

MessageBody.description: The message to send. The minimum size is one character. The maximum size is 256 KiB.

DelaySeconds.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which to delay a specific message. Valid values are 0 to 900.

MessageAttributes.description: Custom message attributes. A maximum of 10 message attributes per message is supported.

MessageSystemAttributes.description: System message attributes to set on the message.

MessageDeduplicationId.description: The token used for deduplication of sent messages. Required for FIFO queues if content-based deduplication is not enabled.

MessageGroupId.description: The tag that specifies that a message belongs to a specific message group. Required for FIFO queues.

SendMessageResponse.title: Send Message Response
SendMessageResponse.description: The response from the SendMessage API action.

MessageId.description: An attribute containing the MessageId of the message sent to the queue.

MD5OfMessageBody.description: An MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message body string.

MD5OfMessageAttributes.description: An MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message attribute string.

MD5OfMessageSystemAttributes.description: An MD5 digest of the non-URL-encoded message system attribute string.

SequenceNumber.description: This parameter applies only to FIFO queues. The large, non-consecutive number that Amazon SQS assigns to each message.

SendMessageBatchRequestEntry.title: Send Message Batch Request Entry
SendMessageBatchRequestEntry.description: Contains the details of a single Amazon SQS message along with an identifier.

Id.description: An identifier for a message in this batch used to communicate the result. The Ids of the batch entries must be unique within a request.

SendMessageBatchRequest.title: Send Message Batch Request
SendMessageBatchRequest.description: The request payload for sending up to 10 messages to an SQS queue in a single batch.

DeleteMessageRequest.title: Delete Message Request
DeleteMessageRequest.description: The request payload for deleting a message from an SQS queue.

ReceiptHandle.description: The receipt handle associated with the message to delete.

DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry.title: Delete Message Batch Request Entry
DeleteMessageBatchRequestEntry.description: Encloses a receipt handle and an identifier for it.

Id.description: An identifier for this particular receipt handle used to communicate the result.

ChangeMessageVisibilityRequest.title: Change Message Visibility Request
ChangeMessageVisibilityRequest.description: The request payload for changing the visibility timeout of a message in an SQS queue.

ReceiptHandle.description: The receipt handle associated with the message whose visibility timeout is changed.

VisibilityTimeout.description: The new value for the message's visibility timeout (in seconds).

QueueAttributes.title: Queue Attributes
QueueAttributes.description: The configurable and reportable attributes of an SQS queue.

QueueArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the queue.

ApproximateNumberOfMessages.description: The approximate number of messages available for retrieval from the queue.

ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible.description: The approximate number of messages that are in flight (sent to a client but not yet deleted or not yet past visibility timeout).

ApproximateNumberOfMessagesDelayed.description: The approximate number of messages in the queue that are delayed and not available for reading immediately.

CreatedTimestamp.description: The time when the queue was created (epoch time in seconds).

LastModifiedTimestamp.description: The time when the queue was last changed (epoch time in seconds).

VisibilityTimeout.description: The visibility timeout for the queue, in seconds. Valid values are 0 to 43200 (12 hours). Default is 30.

MaximumMessageSize.description: The limit of how many bytes a message can contain. Valid values are 1024 to 262144 (1 KiB to 256 KiB). Default is 262144.

MessageRetentionPeriod.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS retains a message. Valid values are 60 to 1209600 (1 minute to 14 days). Default is 345600 (4 days).

DelaySeconds.description: The default delay on the queue, in seconds. Valid values are 0 to 900 (15 minutes). Default is 0.

ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which the ReceiveMessage action waits for a message to arrive. Valid values are 0 to 20. Default is 0 (short polling).

Policy.description: The queue's policy as a JSON string. A valid AWS policy.

RedrivePolicy.description: The string that includes the parameters for the dead-letter queue functionality as a JSON object.

RedriveAllowPolicy.description: The string that includes the parameters for the permissions to the dead-letter queue redrive permission as a JSON object.

FifoQueue.description: Whether the queue is FIFO. Set to true to designate a queue as FIFO.

ContentBasedDeduplication.description: Whether content-based deduplication is enabled for the FIFO queue.

DeduplicationScope.description: Specifies whether message deduplication occurs at the message group or queue level.

FifoThroughputLimit.description: Specifies whether the FIFO queue throughput quota applies to the entire queue or per message group.

KmsMasterKeyId.description: The ID of an AWS managed customer master key (CMK) for Amazon SQS or a custom CMK.

KmsDataKeyReusePeriodSeconds.description: The length of time, in seconds, for which Amazon SQS can reuse a data key to encrypt or decrypt messages. Valid values are 60 to 86400 (1 minute to 24 hours). Default is 300 (5 minutes).

SqsManagedSseEnabled.description: Whether server-side encryption (SSE) is enabled using SQS-owned encryption keys.

LambdaEventSourceMappingRecord.title: Lambda SQS Event Source Mapping Record
LambdaEventSourceMappingRecord.description: A single SQS message record as received by an AWS Lambda function through an event source mapping.

messageId.description: A unique identifier for the message.

receiptHandle.description: An identifier associated with the act of receiving the message.

attributes.description: A map of system attributes associated with the message.

md5OfBody.description: An MD5 digest of the message body.

md5OfMessageAttributes.description: An MD5 digest of the message attributes.

eventSourceARN.description: The ARN of the SQS queue.

awsRegion.description: The AWS region where the SQS queue is located.

LambdaSQSEvent.title: Lambda SQS Event
LambdaSQSEvent.description: The complete event payload that AWS Lambda receives from an SQS event source mapping. Contains an array of one or more SQS message records.

Records.description: An array of SQS message records.
